# SSH Tunnel Manager v0.1.10

## Release Summary

v0.1.10 is a substantial product and release-engineering update. This release introduces a redesigned onboarding flow, a branded Windows EXE installer, expanded platform packaging for AppImage and Windows NSIS builds, stronger SSH credential handling, and a tighter CI quality gate with Qodana and CodeQL coverage.

## Highlights

### Redesigned onboarding and shell experience
- Added a dedicated onboarding window and first-run flow for setup guidance.
- Refined the main shell layout, modal system, and profile interactions.
- Improved command preview, profile editing, filtering, and settings UI structure.

### Branded Windows installer
- Switched Windows packaging to NSIS EXE installers.
- Added a custom installer template and branded assets so the installer visual style aligns with the app's intro guide card.
- Kept standard installer capabilities intact, including upgrade, uninstall, and Windows packaging compatibility.

### Multi-platform packaging improvements
- Added Windows-specific Tauri config for NSIS packaging.
- Expanded release workflow coverage for:
  - macOS Apple Silicon and Intel
  - Linux ARM64 and x86_64 AppImage
  - Windows ARM64 and x86_64 EXE installers
- Improved release validation by enforcing version sync against release tags before packaging.

### SSH and system integration improvements
- Added onboarding, menu bar, windowing, and system command modules in the Tauri backend.
- Refactored SSH process handling and askpass generation into clearer backend modules.
- Hardened credential handling:
  - removed dev-mode password persistence in local storage
  - tightened askpass file permissions
  - avoided plaintext password staging on Unix askpass flows

### Code quality and CI
- Added Qodana workflow and project configuration for frontend quality analysis.
- Kept CodeQL coverage for security-oriented analysis across JavaScript/TypeScript, Rust, and GitHub Actions.
- Resolved review feedback from automated security and code review tooling before release.

## Fixes and polish
- Updated installer branding text to match the current product name.
- Reduced deprecated API usage in clipboard-related frontend flows while preserving compatibility fallbacks.
- Split large frontend and Rust modules into smaller, more maintainable units.
- Improved updater, tunnel polling, SSH detection, and app support utility structure.

## Upgrade Notes
- Existing users should see no configuration migration requirement from this release.
- Windows users now receive an EXE installer instead of the previous MSI-oriented packaging flow.
- Release automation expects version files to match the `v0.1.10` tag exactly.
